adversarial.note:
VERIFIED — claim holds. Could not refute. All four in-scope secrets use the fail-fast ${VAR:?msg} form with ZERO ':-' defaults in /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/docker-compose.yml: JWT_SECRET (lines 272, 365, 405), SIGNING_SECRET (273, 366, 406), INTERNAL_API_KEY (279, 364, 412-413 incl. API_INTERNAL_KEY alias), FRONTEND_SECRET (339). Each errors with a 'set X in .env — generate with openssl rand -hex 32' message. No '-change-me' default on any of them.

Adversarial grep of the whole file: the only ':-' defaults are on NON-secret config (POSTGRES_DB/USER, OLLAMA_*, registry/tag, model names, ports, MCP enable flags, empty-string passthroughs for optional cloud creds). POSTGRES_PASSWORD also correctly fails-fast via ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:?...} (lines 185, 354).

OUT-OF-SCOPE flag (not a refutation): line 276 ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD: ${ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD:-changeme-on-first-login} — a 'changeme' seed-password default still exists, but it is NOT one of the four secrets named in the claim, and ADMIN_REQUIRE_PASSWORD_RESET (line 287) is a paired control. Worth a POA&M line but does not refute this specific claim.

CLAIM HOLDS — could not refute. Read the actual get_user_info (main.py:676-868) and traced every return that sets is_admin=True; all are gated by real verification:

1. No-creds + ENABLE_AUTH=true (695-699): raises HTTPException 401 "missing_authorization". Never returns admin. HTTPBearer is configured auto_error=False (main.py:493), so a missing/malformed Authorization header yields credentials=None and routes into this fail-closed branch. Directly covered by tests (test_jwt_auth.py:146, test_auth_hardening.py:139 asserts 401 and NOT a dict with is_admin).
2. No-creds + ENABLE_AUTH=false (700-710): returns is_admin=True, but this is the explicit, documented local-dev escape gated on auth being disabled — not a bypass under the claim's "(when ENABLE_AUTH)" condition.
3. oa_sys_ token (719-736): is_admin=True is reachable ONLY inside `if verify_system_token(token)`. verify_system_token (147-163) requires startswith(oa_sys_), then fails closed if INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET is unset/empty (159-161 → return False), then recomputes base64url(HMAC_SHA256(secret, "openagentic-system-token")) and compares with hmac.compare_digest (constant-time). A forged Bearer oa_sys_<anything> fails and hits an explicit 401 (732-736) — it does NOT fall through to the weaker oa_ user path. No prefix-trust.
4. oa_ user key (741-768): admin only when the api's /api/auth/me returns 200 AND isAdmin:true; failure falls through, never fabricates admin.
5. Raw API_INTERNAL_KEY match (772-784): returns is_admin=True but gated on `token and token == api_internal_key`; with the env unset api_internal_key='' and a truthy token can never equal '' — unreachable / fails closed.
6. Internal HS256 JWT (799-848): admin only from a jwt.decode verified against JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET with algorithms=['HS256']; fails closed (401) when no secret configured (809-815). Any kid-bearing/other token shape → 401 (857-860); generic exception → 401 (865-868).

No path grants is_admin=True without verification. Caveat: I could not re-execute the pytest suite here (fastapi not installed in this sandbox; ModuleNotFoundError), so the 19/19 green is taken on the externally-reported build state, not re-run by me — but the source itself, plus the present, on-point tests (test_jwt_auth.py:172 oa_sys_ bad-suffix→401, test_auth_hardening.py:153 no-creds-never-admin), confirm the claim.

adversarial.ref: Claim: NO live AAD/OBO/Google-SSO code remains (local-auth only) — refute via grep for AzureADAuth/AzureOBOService/exchange_token_for_azure/googleAuth/msal in services/*/src, excluding comments/prisma/LLM libs.
adversarial.note:
VERDICT on the exact named test: holds=true. None of the 5 named identifiers (AzureADAuth, AzureOBOService, exchange_token_for_azure, googleAuth, msal) appear as LIVE code in services/*/src.

Every grep hit for the 5 identifiers is excluded:
- services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserAuthService.ts:210-218 — MSAL is COMMENTED-OUT (`// const msalInstance = new ConfidentialClientApplication(...)`); the live code just simulates a refresh. Not live.
- services/openagentic-ui/src/app/providers/AuthContext.tsx:244,251 — `'msal.token'` and `key.includes('msal')` are DEFENSIVE logout localStorage-cleanup keys (purging stale Azure keys), not live AAD/MSAL auth. No MSAL import, no ConfidentialClientApplication, no token acquisition.
- No file named *azuread*/*obo*/*googleauth*/*msal*; no live `import/from '@azure/msal-node'` (the dep at services/openagentic-api/package.json:63 is DANGLING — declared but never imported); no login.microsoftonline / accounts.google.com / loginRedirect / PublicClientApplication in the UI; no azure/google/sso login routes registered.
- All google-auth-library / GoogleAuth / GoogleVertex* / @azure/identity (ClientSecretCredential, DefaultAzureCredential) hits are LLM-provider auth (Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, Azure AI Foundry) — explicitly excluded by the claim and legitimately local-auth-orthogonal.

HOWEVER — MATERIAL COUNTER-EVIDENCE outside the 5 named identifiers (flagging honestly, NOT a refutation of the literal test): a fully LIVE Azure-AD token-validation branch survives the excision in services/openagentic-api/src/routes/chat/middleware/auth.middleware.ts. Line 301 rejects tokens lacking `payload.oid`; line 412 `if (decoded.oid)` enters a live 'Processing Azure AD user' path (line 426) that opens a pg Pool and runs `SELECT ... FROM users WHERE azure_oid = $1` (line 443), maps Azure AD groups→isAdmin, and builds a user payload from Azure claims (decoded.oid/preferred_username/upn/groups), including an un-synced fallback at lines 474-496 referencing `/api/auth/azure/sync`. This is live federated-identity middleware reachable by any token carrying an `oid` claim. It does not match any of the 5 grep terms, so the literal claim still holds, but the broader 'OSS is LOCAL-AUTH ONLY' assertion is NOT fully achieved — this branch should be excised (and the azure_oid column drop is correctly already DEFERRED in the POA&M). Recommend a follow-up to remove the auth.middleware.ts Azure branch and drop the dangling @azure/msal-node dependency.

adversarial.ref: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:201 (POST /login, bcrypt verify at :246); services/openagentic-api/src/plugins/auth.plugin.ts:37-43 (localAuthRoutes always registered, prefix /api/auth/local); services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:865 (authPlugin registered unconditionally)
adversarial.note: CONFIRMED, could not refute. (1) local-auth.ts:201 defines fastify.post('/login') with bcrypt credential verification via verifyPassword (bcrypt.compare, local-auth.ts:12,73-94,246). (2) auth.plugin.ts:37-43 imports and registers localAuthRoutes with prefix '/api/auth/local' UNCONDITIONALLY — no feature flag/SSO gate; the try/catch only logs on error. Comment at :35-36 explicitly: "the OSS edition is local-auth-only, so username/password login at /api/auth/local/* is ALWAYS registered". Resolves to POST /api/auth/local/login. (3) server.ts:864-867 registers authPlugin inside registerAllRoutes() with no conditional guard; the authProvider option passed (process.env.AUTH_PROVIDER||'azure-ad') is unused for the local-route registration decision. Login path also has a JWT_SECRET||process.env fallback (local-auth.ts:286) so it survives Vault-not-configured. NON-BLOCKING residue (not part of claim, does not break login): the login handler still POSTs to the MCP orchestrator 'user-login' endpoint to spawn an Azure MCP (local-auth.ts:344-400) — vestigial coupling to the excised Azure surface, wrapped in try/catch so it cannot fail login; and the forced-password-change branch signs with raw module-level JWT_SECRET without the env fallback (local-auth.ts:662). Neither affects the standard POST /api/auth/local/login bcrypt path.

CONFIRMED — cannot refute. The HS256 internal-JWT signer branch is present and live in buildMcpProxyHeaders() in /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/services/openagentic-api/src/services/buildChatV2Deps.ts.

Evidence:
- L529: `const isApiKeyAuth = authMethod === 'api-key';`
- L531: `if (userId) {` — gates the local-auth / api-key user branch (ctx.user is populated from the decoded local JWT per docstring L500-511).
- L533: `const jwtSecret = process.env.JWT_SECRET || process.env.SIGNING_SECRET;` — shared secret with mcp-proxy.
- L539-551: `const jwt = (await import('jsonwebtoken')).default; const internalToken = jwt.sign({ userId, email, name, isAdmin, groups, source: isApiKeyAuth ? 'api-key-internal' : 'local-internal' }, jwtSecret, { expiresIn: '5m' });`
- L552: `headers['Authorization'] = \`Bearer ${internalToken}\`;`

Algorithm = HS256: the sign() options bag is `{ expiresIn: '5m' }` with NO `algorithm` override, and the secret is a string, so jsonwebtoken defaults to HS256 — matching the mcp-proxy verifier which the campaign pins to HS256 (and the L532 inline comment 'Internal HS256 JWT'). The branch is reachable for local chat (the only auth mode in OSS) and emits the Bearer header the mcp-proxy validates with the same secret.

The same buildMcpProxyHeaders() is consumed by both makeExecuteMcpToolWithResolver (L640) and makeExecuteMcpTool (L692), so the signer applies on the live chat→mcp-proxy tool-call path. The else-if/else fallbacks (userToken passthrough L561-563, anonymous API_INTERNAL_KEY L564-567) do not displace the userId branch when a local user is present.

Note (not a refutation of THIS claim): when jwtSecret is unset the branch silently falls back to API_INTERNAL_KEY (L553-560) and when userId is absent it goes anonymous — but for an authenticated local-auth chat user with JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET configured (the standard OSS compose wiring per CLAUDE.md gotcha #6), the HS256 signer branch executes as claimed.

REFUTED. Not every jwt.verify pins algorithms. Counter-evidence: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/admin-mcp-logs.ts:359-362 calls jwt.default.verify(token, secret) with NO third options argument at all — no { algorithms: [...] }. This is a live auth-decision path: the SSE handler GET /api/admin/mcp/logs/stream (line 345) takes the JWT from a query param (EventSource can't send headers), verifies it here, and gates admin access on decoded.isAdmin (line 364). An unpinned HS256-secret verify is exactly the algorithm-confusion / alg-flexibility footgun control C1 is meant to close.

Why the campaign missed it / why the guard is green-but-wrong: the C1 regression test (src/__tests__/architecture/jwt-algorithms-pinned.source-regression.test.ts:49) detects call sites with the regex /\bjwt\.verify\s*\(/, which matches "jwt.verify(" but NOT the "jwt.default.verify(" form used at admin-mcp-logs.ts:359 (the module is loaded via `await import('jsonwebtoken')` then called as jwt.default.verify). So the offending site is invisible to the guard and the test passes. The same blind spot would miss any aliased/namespaced verify.

The other 12 jwt.verify sites I opened ARE correctly pinned: tokenValidator.ts:70, memory-vector/contexts.ts:28, chat/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:192, local-auth.ts:107, auth.ts:352/451/490/547, analytics-monitoring/prompt-metrics.ts:29, advanced-prompting/prompts.ts:37 all pin ['HS256'] inline; TeamsIntegrationService.ts:170 pins ['RS256'] (multi-line) and utils/auth-validator.ts:124 pins ['RS256'] (multi-line). UserAuthService.ts uses decode() (no verify, not in scope). jwt.sign sites are not relevant. But one unpinned reachable verify is enough: holds=false.

Fix: add { algorithms: ['HS256'] } as the 3rd arg at admin-mcp-logs.ts:359, AND broaden the guard regex to catch (jwt|[A-Za-z_$][\w$]*)\.(default\.)?verify\( so aliased/namespaced verify calls can't slip through again.

nist.family: AC (Access Control) — NIST 800-53 High baseline; FedRAMP-readiness evidence for the OpenAgentic OSS launch branch (oss-launch/a3-fedramp). Evidence verified by direct file read on-branch; build state taken on faith (mcp-proxy pytest could not be re-run here — fastapi not installed in this sandbox).

controls.title: Account Management
controls.implementation: Local accounts are managed in the `user` Prisma table; admin-managed CRUD with isAdmin/groups/isActive flags and force_password_change. Login at services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:201-447 looks up by email (line 224), bcrypt-verifies (line 246), and enforces force_password_change on first login (line 263-273 → HTTP 403 requiresPasswordChange). Account disable is honored: only active users with a password_hash authenticate (line 230). Sessions are first-class rows (userSession) created on login (line 306-325) and expired/cleaned hourly (cleanupSessions, line 781-805). Initial admin is seeded by install/wizard. One-shot magic-boot autologin is narrowly scoped and single-use (local-auth.ts:162-195: token consumed in memory, only for the seeded admin).

controls.title: Automated System Account Management
controls.implementation: Session lifecycle is automated: expired sessions are deactivated by setInterval cleanup (local-auth.ts:781-805) and last_accessed_at is updated per request (local-auth.ts:127-135). API keys carry expires_at and is_active and are filtered at validation (tokenValidator.ts:146-165). GAP: no automated disable of inactive *user* accounts (only sessions/keys expire); account inactivity disable is operator-manual. Acceptable for a local-auth small-team baseline but should be noted in SSP.

controls.title: Inactivity Logout / Session Expiry
controls.implementation: JWTs are minted with 24h expiry (local-auth.ts:299, :187, :663). Server-side sessions independently expire at 24h (expiresAt set local-auth.ts:303-304) and are gated on every protected call by verifyToken, which rejects sessions past expires_at or is_active=false (local-auth.ts:110-124 → 401 'Session expired or invalid'). Hourly sweep deactivates expired sessions (local-auth.ts:783-799). jwt.verify pins algorithms:['HS256'] (local-auth.ts:107, tokenValidator.ts:70) so an alg-confusion/none token cannot bypass expiry.

controls.title: Access Enforcement
controls.implementation: Single source-of-truth token validator (tokenValidator.ts:47-131) is the trust root: local HS256 JWT verified against JWT_SECRET with algorithms:['HS256'] pinned (line 70), rejects tokens missing userId (line 72), derives roles from isAdmin (line 89). Enforced via unifiedAuthHook (middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:252) used by authMiddleware (401 on failure, line 358-368). Admin routes are guarded uniformly: admin.plugin.ts wraps every sub-route child instance with preHandler adminMiddleware (lines 83,96,113,124,…), and adminMiddleware returns 403 'Administrator access required' for non-admins (unifiedAuth.ts:373-383). Role-gate authorize() returns 403 on insufficient roles (middleware/authorization.ts:34-40). The mcp-proxy independently re-enforces at the tool boundary (see AC-3 mcp-proxy / IA-2).

controls.title: Access Enforcement at the MCP Tool Boundary — Fail Closed
controls.implementation: services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py get_user_info (line 676) is a FastAPI Depends on EVERY tool path (/mcp line 877, batch 1098, call 1255, list 1378-1388, exec 1625/1801). FAIL-CLOSED on no credentials: with ENABLE_AUTH (default 'true', line 83) a missing Authorization header raises HTTP 401 'missing_authorization' (line 690-699); only the explicit ENABLE_AUTH=false dev path yields a local-admin context (line 700-710). The prior prefix-trust bypass is closed: an `oa_sys_` token is HMAC-verified (verify_system_token, line 147-163) using hmac.compare_digest against base64url(HMAC_SHA256(INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET, label)); a forged `oa_sys_<anything>` fails and is rejected 401 (line 732-736), and an unset INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET rejects (line 158-161). Internal HS256 JWTs are verified against the shared secret with algorithms=['HS256'] (line 819-824) and fail closed when no secret is configured (line 809-815). Unsupported/kid-bearing tokens are rejected 401 (line 859-860). Confirmed by static read; the campaign's 19/19 pytest (test_auth_hardening.py, test_jwt_auth.py) could not be independently re-run in this sandbox (fastapi not installed) — recommend capturing that run in the SSP evidence package.

controls.title: Information Flow Enforcement (CORS / cross-origin)
controls.implementation: API CORS (config/fastify.config.ts:181-204) uses a function origin-checker doing EXACT allow-list match (line 187 allowedOrigins.includes(origin)), never '*'; non-listed origins are rejected with 'Not allowed by CORS' (line 189). credentials:true with explicit method (line 194) and header allow-lists (line 195-203). Default origins are internal service + localhost dev only (line 159-169), overridable via ALLOWED_ORIGINS. mcp-proxy CORS (main.py:461-489) defaults to internal Docker-network services ONLY (openagentic-api/openagentic-ui, line 470-472) when ALLOWED_ORIGINS unset, with explicit allow_methods=['GET','POST','OPTIONS'] (line 481) and an explicit header allow-list (line 482-485) — no wildcard with credentials. Note: the API origin checker allows requests with no Origin (cb(null,true), line 184) for curl/server-to-server — this is standard and not a CORS-bypass (CORS protects browsers; non-browser callers still require a valid bearer token via AC-3).

controls.title: Least Privilege
controls.implementation: Per-user/group permission model in services/openagentic-api/src/services/UserPermissionsService.ts: resolves user→group→default precedence (getUserPermissions, line 125-235) for allowed/denied LLM providers, allowed/denied MCP servers, workflow access, and feature flags. Enforcement helpers canAccessMcpServer (line 492-507: explicit-deny wins, then allow-list) and canAccessLlmProvider (line 472-487). Roles are minimal — only 'admin' is derived (tokenValidator.ts:89); everything else is default-user. Admin-only surface is uniformly gated (adminMiddleware, AC-3). Inter-service principals are scoped: the api→proxy service token grants a service-account/system context (main.py:773-784) rather than a human admin session.

controls.title: Least Privilege — Review / Read-Only Mode for cloud MCPs
controls.implementation: A read-only enforcement layer exists in the mcp-proxy: is_tool_blocked_in_read_only (main.py:242-277) blocks mutating verbs (BLOCKED_TOOL_PATTERNS line 214-229 + DELETE methods line 232) for cloud servers (AWS/Azure/GCP/K8s, READ_ONLY_AFFECTED_SERVERS line 201-210), invoked on every tool/exec path (main.py:946,1657,1834). However it is DISABLED BY DEFAULT (MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE default 'false', main.py:175).
controls.poam: C6 — flip MCP_READ_ONLY default to ON (deferred product decision). Until then least-privilege for destructive cloud ops relies on the separate HITL mutating-approval gate (tool-execution.helper.ts:1984-2043), which IS active and audits+gates mutating calls. Remaining action: ship C6 (MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE=true default) before authorizing cloud-mutating MCPs in a High environment.

controls.title: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
controls.implementation: Failed logons are uniformly rejected with a generic 401 'Invalid credentials' (local-auth.ts:242,259 — no user-enumeration delta) and every failure is persisted to the auth audit log via logAuthEvent with reason, ip, user-agent (local-auth.ts:232-241, 249-258). Rate limiting is registered globally (fastify.config.ts:221-289) and applies to the unauthenticated /login surface by IP (keyGenerator falls back to request.ip, line 247-249). GAP: there is no per-account lockout/throttle threshold tied to consecutive failures (AC-7 expects a configurable max-attempts + lockout). Recommend adding account-level failed-attempt lockout to fully satisfy AC-7 for High.

controls.title: System Use Notification
controls.implementation: No system-use banner is configured in the OSS build. N/A for the platform code baseline — system-use notification for a FedRAMP deployment is an operator/deployment-layer responsibility (login banner injected by the deploying agency), not a control the OSS application code is expected to hard-code.

controls.title: Session Termination
controls.implementation: Logout (local-auth.ts:453-535) deactivates ALL active sessions for the user (updateMany is_active=false, line 461-469) and persists a logout audit event (line 472-480). Subsequent requests with the same JWT are rejected because verifyToken requires an active, unexpired session row (local-auth.ts:110-124). Sessions also auto-terminate at 24h expiry (AC-2(5)). Logout additionally tears down per-user MCP instances (line 482-528).

controls.title: Permitted Actions Without Identification or Authentication
controls.implementation: Only health/metrics and the login/magic endpoints are reachable without a token; all data and tool surfaces require a validated token (authMiddleware → 401, unifiedAuth.ts:358-368) and the mcp-proxy fails closed on missing auth (main.py:690-699). Health/metrics are explicitly carved out of logging/rate-limit only (fastify.config.ts:61-64, allowList line 263), not out of data access. No anonymous data path was found.

controls.title: Federated Identity / Delegated-Token Controls (AAD IdP, OBO, MFA-freshness, Azure-group claims, Google SSO)
controls.implementation: The OSS edition is LOCAL-AUTH ONLY — AAD IdP, On-Behalf-Of token exchange, and Google SSO were excised. There is no federated-identity or delegated-token surface to assess: the mcp-proxy explicitly rejects kid-bearing/RS256 (federated) tokens fail-closed (main.py:857-860), and CSP MCPs use static Service-Account credentials, not OBO. Therefore MFA-freshness, OBO replay, and external-IdP group-claim controls are N/A by design. CAVEAT (residue, not a federation surface): AZURE_* env vars survive ONLY as Azure OpenAI *LLM-provider* auth (UniversalEmbeddingService.ts:307-315; azureOpenAIConfigService.ts:193-194) — an outbound model endpoint, not an inbound identity provider; and local-auth.ts:344-400 still calls an MCP orchestrator 'user-login' to spawn an Azure MCP — that is the Service-Principal spawn path keyed on the LOCAL user's isAdmin (line 369), not OBO/AAD identity, so it does not reintroduce a federated-identity surface.

controls.title: Trust-root / credential fail-closed at boot
controls.implementation: Secrets validation fails CLOSED in production: validateSecret throws on missing or weak/placeholder values when NODE_ENV=production (config/secrets.config.ts:76-138 — exact + substring placeholder blocklists incl. 'change-me','dev-','placeholder'), and the bootstrap step re-raises to abort boot (startup/01-secrets.ts:20-24, critical:true). JWT_SECRET placeholder is caught (tokenValidator.ts:19-27) and the mcp-proxy bootstrap_jwt_keys rejects missing/'dev-secret' keys (main.py:100-126). MATERIAL FINDING / GAP: secrets.config.ts:168 and :170 still require AZURE_CLIENT_ID and AZURE_TENANT_ID as MANDATORY (validateSecret WITHOUT allowEmpty). In a local-auth-only production deploy using Ollama/non-Azure LLM providers these are unset, so loadSecrets() THROWS and the critical 01-secrets step ABORTS BOOT — a fail-closed crash on a legitimate, supported configuration. This is residue of the AAD excision: those vars are now only meaningful for the optional Azure OpenAI provider. Action: make AZURE_CLIENT_ID/AZURE_TENANT_ID conditional (allowEmpty / gated on AZURE OpenAI provider selection) so a local-auth, non-Azure prod deploy boots.

controls.title: Auditing of access decisions (supports AC-3/AC-6 enforcement)
controls.implementation: Every tool call is audited and mutating calls are gated: tool-execution.helper.ts:1989-2043 classifies each tool (classifyTool), inserts an immutable audit row (insertAuditRow) for READ/auto and, when policy.gateMutating is on, persists a 'pending' row, emits 'approval_required', and awaits the ApprovalRegistry (timeout→deny, denies the call line 2029-2039). Auth events (login/logout/failed) persist via authAuditLogger (local-auth.ts:232,249,333,472; service at services/openagentic-api/src/services/audit/authAuditLogger.ts). API-key usage is logged to AdminAuditLog (unifiedAuth.ts:313-329). Note: HITL risk-gate on the same path is fail-OPEN on internal error (tool-execution.helper.ts:1980-1982) — acceptable since the independent mutating-approval gate below it is fail-closed, but flag for SSP.

controls.title: Deferred remediation (tracked POA&M)
controls.implementation: Not an AC control directly; tracked as deferred per campaign scope.
controls.poam: C10 — de-duplicate the forked workflow-engine. Remaining action: consolidate the Flowise-derived engine fork; no AC impact asserted but listed for completeness.

controls.implementation: GroupPermissions retains azure_group_id/azure_group_name columns (UserPermissionsService.ts:62-65, :352, schema) and code paths still reference them even though federated identity is excised. They are presently inert (no AAD code populates them in local-auth-only).

nist.family: AU — Audit and Accountability (NIST 800-53 High baseline)

controls.title: Event Logging
controls.implementation: The platform logs the security-relevant event classes required by High baseline across dedicated audit tables, all unioned by services/audit/activityAggregator.ts:160-490 (one UNION ALL over tool-call, user-query, admin, credential, flow, agent, webhook, synth, DLP, workflow-execution/-approval, and auth sources). Specifically: (a) Authentication events — login/login_failed/logout via services/audit/authAuditLogger.ts:51-72 writing auth_audit_log, invoked at routes/local-auth.ts:232 (failed: unknown user), :249 (failed: bad password), :333 (login success), :472 (logout). (b) Tool/command execution — EVERY chat tool call audited via runAuditAndGate at three convergence seams: routes/chat/pipeline/chat/dispatchTool.ts:204, pipeline/built-in-hooks.ts:214, and the mcp-proxy executor wrap services/buildChatV2Deps.ts:1233 (auditMcpExecutionSeam), all writing tool_call_audit_log via services/approval/auditLog.ts:28-45. (c) Admin/config actions — admin_audit_log written from routes/admin/permissions.ts:66, admin/v3-extras.ts:118, admin/v3-extras-mutations.ts:62, chat/user-data-management.ts:102/309, utils/auditTrail.ts:143, and API-key usage at middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:313. (d) MCP tool exec also dual-logged to user_query_audit at routes/chat/pipeline/tool-execution.helper.ts:571.

controls.title: Content of Audit Records
controls.implementation: Records capture the AU-3 required fields — what/when/where/who/outcome/source. tool_call_audit_log (schema.prisma:1595-1617) stores tool_name, server_name, args(JSON), preview, classification(READ/MUTATING), decision, decided_by, decided_at, user_id, session_id, message_id, origin, created_at. auth_audit_log (schema.prisma:1305-1323) stores event, provider, success, user_id, user_email, ip_address, user_agent, detail(JSON), created_at — i.e. outcome (success bool), source (ip/user-agent), identity (attempted email even on failure, see authAuditLogger.ts:36-42). admin_audit_log (schema.prisma:1275-1294) stores admin_user_id, admin_email, action, resource_type, resource_id, details(JSON), ip_address, created_at. user_query_audit (schema.prisma:1354+) stores raw_query, query_type, intent, ip_address, user_agent, mcp_server, tools_called, tool_results, response_time_ms, success, error_code/message, model_used, tokens_consumed. args preview is bounded to 500 chars (auditLog.ts:4-13) to avoid unbounded payloads.

controls.title: Additional Audit Information
controls.implementation: Records include extended context beyond the minimum: AuthAuditEntry.detail carries structured failure reasons (local-auth.ts:240 'unknown_user_or_no_password', :257 'invalid_password', :341 isAdmin flag); tool-call records carry full args + a bounded preview + classification + approval decision + decided_by; user_query_audit carries model_used, tokens_consumed, cost_estimate, response_time_ms, and tool_results (AuditLogger.ts:199-229).

controls.title: Time Stamps
controls.implementation: Every audit table stamps created_at via the database server clock using Prisma @default(now()) — tool_call_audit_log (schema.prisma:1609), auth_audit_log (schema.prisma:1315), admin_audit_log (schema.prisma:1284), user_query_audit. Terminal approval decisions stamp decided_at = new Date() at auditLog.ts:60. Using the single Postgres now() source gives a consistent, server-authoritative time base rather than per-client clocks; timestamps are stored as DateTime (UTC) and exported ISO-8601 (admin-audit-logs.ts CSV).

controls.title: Protection of Audit Information
controls.implementation: tool_call_audit_log is enforced append-only at the application layer: the immutability invariant is pinned by the source-regression cage __tests__/architecture/audit-log-append-only.source-regression.test.ts:40-79, which fails the build if ANY toolCallAuditLog.delete/deleteMany exists, or if any toolCallAuditLog.update* exists outside the single guarded updateMany in services/approval/auditLog.ts:58-62 (WHERE decision:'pending' — a row can transition pending→decided exactly once and never be re-mutated or deleted). Read access to all audit feeds is admin-gated: admin-audit-logs.ts:110/132/174/208/264 attach onRequest: adminMiddleware. The decide endpoint is auth-gated (server.ts:977, chat.plugin.ts:154 addHook authMiddleware).
controls.poam: GAP (not a deferred item, surfaced by this audit): the append-only guarantee is APP-enforced only — there is no DB-level trigger/REVOKE preventing direct DELETE/UPDATE on the admin schema, and the other audit tables (admin_audit_log, auth_audit_log, user_query_audit) have NO equivalent source cage or immutability guard — only tool_call_audit_log is caged. The approve/deny route (approval-gate.routes.ts:26) authenticates the caller but does NOT verify the approver is an admin or is distinct from the requester (no separation-of-duty check). Recommend tracking a new POA&M item: add DB-level append-only enforcement (revoke UPDATE/DELETE + insert-only role, or pgAudit) and extend the source cage to all four audit tables + add an authz check on the approval decision.

controls.title: Non-repudiation
controls.implementation: A tamper-evident cryptographic hash chain is DEFINED for admin_audit_log: schema.prisma:1286-1291 adds previous_hash + chain_hash columns (SHA-256 over previousHash|eventType|userId|action|timestamp|details) computed in services/AuditLogger.ts:90-107, with a chain-verification routine verifyAdminAuditChain at AuditLogger.ts:130-172 that walks the chain and reports the first broken link.
controls.poam: GAP surfaced by this audit (verified, not assumed): AuditLogger.logAdminAction (the ONLY writer that computes the chain hash) is NEVER invoked in the live path — grep shows zero call sites for AuditLogger / logUserQuery / the chained logAdminAction outside AuditLogger.ts itself; the class is effectively dead code. The admin audit rows that ARE written go through direct prisma.adminAuditLog.create calls (DatabaseService.ts:1491, unifiedAuth.ts:313, permissions.ts:66, v3-extras.ts:118, v3-extras-mutations.ts:62) which set NO previous_hash/chain_hash — so the chain is unpopulated and verifyAdminAuditChain is never called by any route or startup step. Net: the non-repudiation chain is a design that is not wired. Recommend a new POA&M: route all admin-audit writes through the chaining writer and expose verifyAdminAuditChain via an admin integrity-check endpoint + startup verification.

controls.title: Audit Record Generation
controls.implementation: Audit generation is centralized at the seams every event must pass through rather than scattered opportunistically. For tool calls, runAuditAndGate (auditAndGate.ts:88-174) is invoked at the dispatch seam (dispatchTool.ts:204), the before_tool_call hook (built-in-hooks.ts:214), AND the mcp-proxy executor wrap (buildChatV2Deps.ts:1233), with a single-pass AUDIT_DONE_FLAG (auditAndGate.ts:40,66-75) so exactly one row is written per call no matter which seam fires — this closed the prior 'tool executed, audit total:0' bypass. MUTATING calls fail-SAFE: if the pending audit row cannot be inserted the call is BLOCKED (auditAndGate.ts:128-140), so no un-audited mutation can execute. Auth and admin generation wired at the route handlers as cited in AU-2. The aggregator (activityAggregator.ts) provides the unified org-wide record set.

controls.title: Audit Review, Analysis, Reporting & Audit Reduction
controls.implementation: Admin-only review/reduction APIs are mounted (server.ts:1143-1144, prefix /api/admin) in routes/admin-audit-logs.ts: GET /audit-logs unified feed with filters by scope/resourceType/date/success/actor/searchTerm + pagination (admin-audit-logs.ts:108-127); /audit-logs/stats counts by type+outcome with 24h/7d KPI windows (:130-169); /audit-logs/errors failures-only reduction (:172-203); /audit-logs/sessions chat-session feed (:206-259); /audit-logs/export CSV/JSON export of the filtered set (:262-282). The activityAggregator does the audit reduction in-DB via one ordered+sliced UNION ALL across all sources (activityAggregator.ts:634-691).

controls.title: Audit Storage Capacity
controls.implementation: Audit records persist to Postgres (the platform's primary datastore), inheriting its storage management; tables are indexed for query/pagination without table scans (tool_call_audit_log @@index on decision/user_id/tool_name/created_at, schema.prisma:1611-1614; auth_audit_log indexes on created_at desc/event/user_id/success, schema.prisma:1317-1320), and feeds are capped per-request (export capped to 200 rows, admin-audit-logs.ts:269; feed maxLimit 200, :54). Stored payloads are bounded (args preview 500 chars, auditLog.ts:4).

controls.title: Azure-AD / SSO / OBO authentication events
controls.implementation: N/A by design: the OSS edition is local-auth only — AAD IdP, OBO, and Google-SSO were excised (commit 0e20c668b), so there is no federated-identity or delegated-token authentication surface to audit. The auth_audit_log schema retains a generic provider column and an 'sso_login' event enum (authAuditLogger.ts:21-26) for the enterprise edition, but the OSS code emits only provider:'local' events.

controls.title: MCP_READ_ONLY default (deferred product decision)
controls.implementation: Deferred per the remediation plan — affects the breadth of MUTATING vs READ classification surfaced to the approval/audit gate but does not block AU generation.
controls.poam: Remaining: flip MCP_READ_ONLY to default-on (product decision). Tracked as deferred item C6.

controls.title: Forked workflow-engine dedup
controls.implementation: Deferred — the workflows engine fork duplicates some audit-adjacent execution paths (workflow_executions/workflow_approvals are already unioned into the feed, activityAggregator.ts:426/454).
controls.poam: Remaining: de-duplicate the forked workflow engine. Tracked as deferred item C10.

controls.title: Lint-gate flip
controls.implementation: Deferred — CI lint gate not yet enforced as a hard gate.
controls.poam: Remaining: flip lint to a blocking gate. Tracked as deferred item C11.

controls.title: Base-image digest pin
controls.implementation: Deferred — base container images are tag-pinned, not digest-pinned (supply-chain hardening, indirectly supports audit-tooling integrity).
controls.poam: Remaining: pin base images by sha256 digest. Tracked as deferred item C12.

controls.title: Forward-migration to drop unused Azure/SSO columns
controls.implementation: Verified deferred: no Azure/SSO column-drop migration exists under prisma/migrations/ (latest migrations end at 20260524000000_model_fca_column; grep for DROP COLUMN azure/sso returns nothing). The excised-IdP columns remain physically present in the DB.
controls.poam: Remaining: author the forward-only Prisma migration to drop the now-unused Azure/SSO columns (must use migrate deploy per the no-destructive-migration cage, not db push). Tracked as the deferred Prisma item.

nist.family: IA — Identification and Authentication (NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5, FedRAMP High baseline)

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
controls.implementation: Local users authenticate by email+password. POST /api/auth/local/login resolves the user by email and verifies the supplied password against the stored bcrypt hash via bcrypt.compare before issuing any session — services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:224-260 (lookup + verifyPassword at :246, which calls bcrypt.compare at :75). On success a uniquely-identified HS256 JWT carrying userId/email/isAdmin is minted (local-auth.ts:290-300) and a per-token DB session row is created (local-auth.ts:306-325). All authenticated request paths run through the single unified hook unifiedAuthHook (services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:85) which delegates to validateAnyToken (services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:47). NOTE: federated/SSO identity (Azure AD / Google) is N/A by design — this is a local-auth-only edition; there is no IdP/OBO surface to assess.

controls.title: MFA to Privileged/Non-Privileged Accounts (federated MFA-freshness)
controls.implementation: N/A — local-auth-only edition. There is no federated identity provider or delegated-token (OBO) flow, so MFA-freshness / re-authentication assertions tied to an external IdP do not apply. The AAD IdP/OBO/Google-SSO code was excised (commit 0e20c668b). MFA, if required by an authorizing org, is an environmental/IdP-layer responsibility outside this OSS edition's auth surface.

controls.title: Access to Accounts — Replay Resistant (inter-service)
controls.implementation: Inter-service system calls use an HMAC-bound bearer token rather than a guessable prefix. The api mints oa_sys_<base64url(HMAC_SHA256(INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET,"openagentic-system-token"))> — services/openagentic-api/src/services/llm-providers/util/mintInterServiceSystemToken.ts:30-34. The mcp-proxy recomputes the same HMAC and does a constant-time hmac.compare_digest — services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:147-163. The bare prefix is explicitly NEVER trusted: a forged 'Bearer oa_sys_<anything>' fails verify_system_token() and is rejected 401 (main.py:719-736). This closes the pre-B3 prefix-trust bypass (documented in the comment at main.py:151-152).

controls.title: Device / Inter-Service Identification and Authentication
controls.implementation: Three distinct, secret-bound inter-service credential types are recognized and each verified cryptographically before grant: (1) oa_sys_ HMAC system token — constant-time compare (main.py:147-163, gate at :719-736); (2) raw INTERNAL_API_KEY exact match for the api→proxy service path (main.py:772-784, env var, no default); (3) internal HS256 JWT validated against the shared JWT_SECRET/SIGNING_SECRET (main.py:799-855). On the api side, the unifiedAuth internal-service bypass requires a timing-safe match of x-internal-secret against INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET (services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/unifiedAuth.ts:100-107, crypto.timingSafeEqual) — the v0.4.0 IP-only fallback was removed (unifiedAuth.ts:198-206). The internal key is read from a rotation-aware projected-secret file with env fallback (services/openagentic-api/src/utils/internalKeyReader.ts:45-58).

controls.title: Authenticator Management (password & secret hygiene, fail-closed)
controls.implementation: Passwords are stored only as bcrypt hashes (cost 10) — services/openagentic-api/src/routes/local-auth.ts:69-71 (hashPassword) and :638-648 (change-password rehash). API keys are stored as bcrypt hashes and matched with bcrypt.compare, never in plaintext — services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:146-169. Secrets fail CLOSED in production: validateSecret THROWS (aborting boot) on a missing or weak/placeholder secret when NODE_ENV=production, with both exact and substring blocklists that catch the formerly-shipped '...-change-me' and 'dev-' patterns — services/openagentic-api/src/config/secrets.config.ts:76-137 (throw at :82-86; blocklists :101-127). getJWTSecret/getSigningSecret throw when neither JWT_SECRET nor SIGNING_SECRET is set — services/openagentic-api/src/utils/secrets.ts:132-137, :156-161. docker-compose.yml enforces this at the orchestration layer via ${VAR:?} fail-fast on POSTGRES_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET, SIGNING_SECRET, INTERNAL_API_KEY, INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET, FRONTEND_SECRET (docker-compose.yml:32,272-273,279,283,339,364-366,405-418) — no *-change-me defaults remain. The mcp-proxy fails closed when INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET or JWT_SECRET is unset (main.py:158-161 and :809-815). NOTE: tokenValidator.ts:19-23 still generates an ephemeral runtime JWT_SECRET on a placeholder value and logs CRITICAL rather than throwing — but the production fail-closed gate lives in secrets.config.ts (boot abort) and compose ${VAR:?}, so a production deploy cannot reach this codepath with an unset secret; in non-prod it degrades to ephemeral-with-loud-warning.

controls.title: Password-Based Authentication (storage protection, forced rotation)
controls.implementation: Passwords never stored or compared in cleartext — bcrypt only (local-auth.ts:69-94). Forced first-login password change is supported via force_password_change (login returns 403 requiresPasswordChange when ADMIN_REQUIRE_PASSWORD_RESET != 'false' — local-auth.ts:262-281; change-password clears the flag and rehashes — :638-648). Failed verifications are logged without exposing the secret (local-auth.ts:78-82) and recorded as a normalized auth_audit_log row (login_failed) for AU/IA traceability (local-auth.ts:247-258 → services/openagentic-api/src/services/audit/authAuditLogger.ts:51-60).

controls.title: Authentication Feedback & Cryptographic Module Authentication (JWT algorithm pinning)
controls.implementation: Every jwt.verify() in api source pins an explicit algorithms allow-list, defeating the alg:none / algorithm-confusion class. Local + inter-service JWTs are pinned to HS256: tokenValidator.ts:70, local-auth.ts:107, routes/auth.ts:352/451/490/547, routes/chat/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:192, plus prompt-metrics/prompts/contexts routes. The two RS256 verifiers (TeamsIntegrationService.ts:170-181, utils/auth-validator.ts:124-129) also pin algorithms:['RS256']. This is enforced by a source-regression test that fails CI if any unpinned jwt.verify is reintroduced — services/openagentic-api/src/__tests__/architecture/jwt-algorithms-pinned.source-regression.test.ts:39-63. On the proxy, internal HS256 tokens are decoded with algorithms=['HS256'] (main.py:819-824) and any other token shape (e.g. kid-bearing RS256) is rejected fail-closed (main.py:857-860). Authentication feedback is generic ('Invalid credentials', local-auth.ts:242/259) — no user-enumeration leak.

controls.title: Identification and Authentication (Non-Organizational Users) — fail-closed default
controls.implementation: An unauthenticated request can never default to system-admin. In the mcp-proxy, a request with no Authorization header is rejected 401 whenever ENABLE_AUTH is on, and ENABLE_AUTH defaults to true (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:83, gate at :690-699); the no-credential local-admin fallback is reachable ONLY under the explicit ENABLE_AUTH=false dev opt-out (main.py:700-710). On the api, unifiedAuthHook throws (→401) when no token is present in header/query/cookie (unifiedAuth.ts:247-249). The first-run convenience MAGIC_BOOT_TOKEN one-shot autologin is entropy-gated (requires length>=16), single-use (cleared after one exchange), and only mints a token for the seeded admin — local-auth.ts:162-195.

controls.title: Orphan Azure-AD auth middleware still present in source
controls.implementation: FINDING (not a live break): services/openagentic-api/src/middleware/fastify-auth.ts:38 still validates ONLY Azure AD tokens via validateAzureADToken (services/openagentic-api/src/utils/auth-validator.ts:124), and it is imported as a preHandler on 8 routes in services/openagentic-api/src/routes/memories.ts:10,60-445. However routes/memories.ts is a DEAD orphan — its default export `memoriesRoutes` is registered NOWHERE; the live /api/memories surface is served by routes/memory-vector/memories.js via memoryVectorPlugin (plugins/memory-ai.plugin.ts:113 / server.ts:1547), which uses the local-auth unifiedAuth path. So no local user is actually 401'd. Risk: this AAD-coupled chain (fastify-auth.ts + auth-validator.ts) survived the excision as compiled-but-unwired source; if ever mounted it would reject all local users. POA&M remaining action: delete routes/memories.ts + middleware/fastify-auth.ts + utils/auth-validator.ts (and the legacy Azure-MCP-orchestrator spawn calls at local-auth.ts:344-400, which are best-effort and do not gate login), and add these paths to the sync SKIP list to prevent re-introduction. Deferred item id: IA-RESIDUAL-AAD-ORPHAN.

controls.title: Deferred POA&M items affecting IA/CM posture
controls.implementation: Confirmed-present deferred items (correctly NOT counted as fixed): (C6) MCP_READ_ONLY_MODE defaults to false — services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:175; remaining action: product decision on flipping the safe default. (Prisma) now-unused Azure/SSO identity columns physically remain in the schema — azure_oid/azure_tenant_id at services/openagentic-api/prisma/schema.prisma:27-28,188,276,294 (and the 'sso_login' enum literal at :1309); remaining action: forward-only Prisma migration to drop them (no db push). (C10) forked workflow-engine dedup, (C11) lint-gate flip, (C12) base-image digest-pin — all remain open per the campaign; not IA-material but tracked. Deferred item ids: C6, C10, C11, C12, PRISMA-DROP-AAD-COLUMNS.

nist.family: SC — System and Communications Protection (NIST 800-53 Rev 5, High baseline)

controls.title: Boundary Protection (SSRF / egress guard)
controls.implementation: VERIFIED. The one unauthenticated externally-reachable fetch primitive — POST /api/setup/probe-ollama — is both setup-gated and SSRF-guarded. services/openagentic-api/src/routes/setup.ts:98-101 returns 409 once any active admin exists (the route is dead post-bootstrap, removing the long-lived SSRF primitive). services/openagentic-api/src/routes/setup.ts:106-108 calls ssrfReject() BEFORE the fetch; ssrfReject (lines 59-74) rejects any non-http(s) scheme (file://, gopher://) and blocks the cloud-metadata hosts enumerated at lines 50-56 (169.254.169.254, fd00:ec2::254, metadata.google.internal, metadata.goog). Proven green: src/routes/__tests__/setup-probe-ssrf.test.ts ran 5/5 PASS in this session (setup-gate 409, IMDS block, scheme reject, LAN-host allowed pre-setup). PARTIAL because the guard is scoped to this single route: the design intentionally does NOT block all RFC-1918 space (a fresh install legitimately probes host.docker.internal / 192.168.x.x). The broader MCP outbound/tool-call egress path (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py tool dispatch, web MCP) has NO private-IP/metadata allowlist — a chat-driven tool could be steered at internal hosts. That residual egress hardening is not yet implemented.
controls.poam: Residual: no platform-wide outbound-egress allowlist on MCP tool calls / web MCP (only the probe-ollama route is guarded). Recommend a shared egress URL-validator for the mcp-proxy dispatch path. Not in the named C6/C10/C11/C12/prisma deferral set — newly identified here; track as a new POA&M item.

controls.title: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity (TLS)
controls.implementation: Internal service-to-service integrity is enforced cryptographically: inter-service calls carry an HMAC-verified oa_sys_ token (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:719-736, constant-time hmac.compare_digest at line 163) or an HS256-pinned JWT (main.py:819-824, algorithms=['HS256']). However the OSS v1 docker-compose path terminates NO TLS — grep of docker-compose.yml found no caddy/tls/443/cert directives; all listeners are plaintext HTTP on the compose network and the host UI port. TLS termination is expected to be supplied by the downstream deployment (the helm chart / an external ingress), not by the OSS compose stack itself. So in-transit confidentiality at the platform edge is a deployment responsibility, not implemented in-repo for the compose install.
controls.poam: For a FedRAMP boundary the edge must terminate TLS 1.2+; OSS compose ships HTTP-only. Document the TLS-terminating ingress requirement in the SSP and supply it via the helm/ingress layer. Track as deployment-architecture POA&M (no in-repo code item).

controls.title: Cryptographic Protection / Algorithm pinning
controls.implementation: VERIFIED. All JWT verification pins the accepted algorithm, closing the alg-confusion / alg:none class. services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:70, routes/local-auth.ts:107, routes/auth.ts:352 and :451 all call jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET, { algorithms: ['HS256'] }); RS256 paths (TeamsIntegrationService.ts:171, utils/auth-validator.ts:127) pin ['RS256']. The mcp-proxy mirrors this: main.py:822 jwt.decode(..., algorithms=['HS256']). A source-regression guard test enforces it repo-wide: src/__tests__/architecture/jwt-algorithms-pinned.source-regression.test.ts walks all non-test .ts and fails on any unpinned jwt.verify — ran 1/1 PASS in this session.

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Establishment & Use (secret material strength, no weak defaults)
controls.implementation: VERIFIED on two layers. (1) Orchestration fail-fast: docker-compose.yml uses ${VAR:?...} for every secret — POSTGRES_PASSWORD (line 32), JWT_SECRET (272/365/405), SIGNING_SECRET (273/366/406), INTERNAL_API_KEY (279/364/413), INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET (283/418), FRONTEND_SECRET (339) — so a missing secret aborts `docker compose up`; the prior *-change-me defaults are gone. (2) Runtime fail-closed: services/openagentic-api/src/config/secrets.config.ts validateSecret() (lines 76-138) THROWS in NODE_ENV=production (lines 80-85) on a missing OR weak/placeholder value; the substring blocklist (lines 117-127) now catches change-me/changeme/dev-/dev_/placeholder ANYWHERE in the value, closing the prior exact-only gap that let openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me through. The dev-only generated fallbacks (lines 154-187) only fire when NODE_ENV!=production. mcp-proxy refuses to boot without a strong signing key (main.py:110-119) and rejects dev-secret* (line 119).

controls.title: Protection of Information at Rest (secret-at-rest, Vault optional)
controls.implementation: Secret-at-rest is delegated to HashiCorp Vault when configured: services/openagentic-api/src/services/vault.service.ts reads VAULT_TOKEN from env (line 45) with NO hardcoded dev-token fallback in the service; the dev-vault-token convenience default lives only in secrets.config.ts:179-180 and is (a) dev-only and (b) allowEmpty=true so production with Vault disabled simply runs without it. Vault is OPTIONAL — when disabled, secrets live in the orchestrator-supplied env / .env (protected by host filesystem permissions, not application-layer encryption). No application-layer field encryption of secrets persisted to Postgres was found, so at-rest protection for stored credentials relies on the deployment's disk/DB encryption.
controls.poam: At-rest encryption of credentials stored in Postgres is not implemented in-app; relies on deployment disk/DB encryption + optional Vault. Document the Vault-ESO / encrypted-volume requirement in the SSP. New POA&M item (not in the named deferral set).

controls.title: Mobile/Active Code Execution — sandboxing (isolated-vm)
controls.implementation: VERIFIED. Workflow code/condition/transform nodes no longer use new Function(); they execute in a fresh V8 isolate via isolated-vm. services/shared/workflow-engine/src/sandbox.ts:87 new ivm.Isolate({ memoryLimit }); the isolate has no process/globalThis/require/Buffer/Node globals (only explicitly-injected JSON-serialisable globals, lines 96-99), input crosses the boundary as JSON and is re-parsed inside (lines 102-117) so no live host reference leaks, hard wall-time cap (DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS=5000, enforced at eval line 128) and memory cap (DEFAULT_MEMORY_CAP_MB=256, line 71), and isolate.dispose() in finally (lines 148-152). The code node executor hard-fails if the sandbox hook is missing (services/shared/workflow-engine/src/nodes/code/executor.ts: 'ctx.runIsolatedCode hook is required ... sandbox unavailable') and rejects any non-javascript language — there is no escape to in-process eval. NOTE: the standalone synth-executor service has been removed from the OSS edition (no services/synth-executor dir, no compose reference), so the only code-exec surface is this isolated-vm path.

controls.title: Denial of Service Protection (rate limiting)
controls.implementation: @fastify/rate-limit is registered in services/openagentic-api/src/server.ts:714-756 with per-user keying (keyGenerator line 736 uses userId then ip), admin-configurable max via Redis platform:rate_limits (lines 719-734), a 1-minute window, and a 429 errorResponseBuilder (747-751). Internal/health/WebSocket paths are exempted (allowList lines 739-746).

controls.title: CORS origin allow-listing

nist.family: SI — System and Information Integrity (NIST 800-53 Rev. 5, FedRAMP High baseline)

controls.title: Information Input Validation
controls.implementation: jwt.verify() algorithm pinning is the load-bearing input-validation fix that closes the alg-confusion / alg:none class. Every one of the 12 source jwt.verify() call sites pins an HS256 (or RS256 for Teams/JWKS) allow-list — e.g. auth/tokenValidator.ts:70 `jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET, { algorithms: ['HS256'] })`, routes/local-auth.ts:107, routes/auth.ts:352/451/490/547, routes/chat/middleware/auth.middleware.ts:192, plus RS256 pins at services/TeamsIntegrationService.ts:171 and utils/auth-validator.ts:127. A source-regression guard test (__tests__/architecture/jwt-algorithms-pinned.source-regression.test.ts:39-63) walks all of src/ and FAILS the build if any jwt.verify lacks an `algorithms:` option within an 8-line window — so a re-introduced unpinned verify cannot land. Request-body validation also present: setup.ts:133-139 validates adminEmail/password length/ollamaHost scheme before use; utils/validation.ts provides StringValidator (XSS strip, email/UUID/identifier regex, length bounds).

controls.controlId: SI-10 (2) — SSRF / input-sanitization on the unauthenticated probe
controls.title: Input Validation — SSRF guard on setup probe
controls.implementation: The unauthenticated POST /api/setup/probe-ollama is both setup-gated and SSRF-guarded. setup.ts:98-101 dies (HTTP 409) once any active admin exists, so the route is not a permanent SSRF primitive. setup.ts:59-74 ssrfReject() parses the host as a URL, rejects any non-http(s) scheme (blocks file://, gopher://, etc.) and blocks cloud-metadata endpoints (169.254.169.254, AWS IPv6 IMDS fd00:ec2::254, metadata.google.internal) before any fetch; enforced at setup.ts:107-108 prior to the fetch at :111. Covered by routes/__tests__/setup-probe-ssrf.test.ts. Residual (documented in code at :48-49): private/LAN space is intentionally NOT blocked because a legitimate Ollama lives on loopback/LAN — the durable control is the needsSetup gate, not full egress filtering.

controls.controlId: SI-3 / AC-3 (mutating-action approval) — HITL approval gate on mutating tool calls
controls.title: Human-in-the-loop approval gate + immutable audit on mutating tool calls
controls.implementation: Every chat-driven tool call is audited and MUTATING calls pause for human approval. classifyTool() (services/approval/classifyTool.ts) is the single READ/MUTATING classifier (mutating-verb set + read-override + exact-only short-verb collision guard so `postgres`!=`post`). runAuditAndGate() (services/approval/auditAndGate.ts) is the reusable primitive: READ or gate-off → append-only insertAuditRow(decision='auto'); MUTATING + gate-on → insertAuditRow(decision='pending'), emit `approval_required` SSE, await ApprovalRegistry.waitFor(timeout); a non-approved outcome returns allowed=false and the mutation NEVER reaches the executor. CRITICALLY it fails CLOSED: if the pending-row INSERT fails it returns allowed:false / blockReason (auditAndGate.ts ~line: 'pending INSERT failed — blocking mutating call') so an un-audited mutation cannot slip through. Three convergence seams all delegate to it (single-pass via AUDIT_DONE_FLAG): the live chat dispatch (routes/chat/pipeline/chat/dispatchTool.ts:203-226), the mcp-proxy executor wrap auditMcpExecutionSeam wired at services/buildChatV2Deps.ts:1233 (covers V2 discovery-mode MCP execution — the cloud/k8s infra writes), and the before_tool_call built-in hook (pipeline/built-in-hooks.ts:214). The append-only log uses a concurrency-guarded single mutation: decideAuditRow updateMany WHERE decision='pending' (auditLog.ts:53-63) so approve/timeout race resolves exactly once. Approve/deny endpoint at routes/chat/approval-gate.routes.ts:26.

controls.controlId: SI-6 — Security and Privacy Function Verification (fail-closed on errors / secrets)
controls.title: Fail-closed on missing/weak secrets and on secret-load errors
controls.implementation: config/secrets.config.ts validateSecret() (lines 76-138) fails CLOSED in production: when NODE_ENV=production a missing, empty, or placeholder/weak secret THROWS and aborts boot (line 80-85). The blocklist closes the exact gap from the prior audit — substringPlaceholders (lines 117-127) includes the shipped docker-compose `-change-me` suffix and the `dev-`/`dev_` weak convention, not just exact matches, so `openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me` is now rejected. loadSecrets()'s catch re-throws (line 214) so a secret-load failure aborts rather than booting with fabricated secrets. mcp-proxy mirrors this: an internal HS256 token with NO configured shared secret is REJECTED 401 rather than decoded with a default (main.py:806-815, 'Fail closed: with no configured secret we cannot verify'). Caveat (not a control gap, but flagged): the misleading catch-block comment in secrets.config.ts:209-213 says 'Never crash / generating fallback' but the code correctly throws — comment should be corrected to avoid future regression. Note utils/secrets.ts still has empty-string env fallbacks (e.g. getDatabaseCredentials password ''), but that file is the Vault-fallback accessor; the boot-gating authority is secrets.config.ts + secrets-startup step.

controls.controlId: IA-2 / AC-3 — Inter-service identity (mcp-proxy fail-closed, no prefix-trust)
controls.title: Inter-service token auth — HMAC-verified, no-creds rejected, fail-closed
controls.implementation: mcp-proxy no longer trusts the oa_sys_ prefix blindly (the pre-B3 bypass that granted system-root to any `Bearer oa_sys_<anything>`). main.py:719-736: an oa_sys_ token is HMAC-verified via verify_system_token() (hmac.compare_digest against base64url(HMAC_SHA256(INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET,label)), main.py:147-163) and a failed verify is REJECTED 401 (fail closed), not passed through. A request with NO Authorization header is rejected 401 when ENABLE_AUTH is on (main.py:690-699) — local-admin fallback only when auth is explicitly disabled. Internal HS256 JWTs are decoded only with algorithms=['HS256'] (main.py:822) and only when a real shared secret exists (main.py:809-815).

controls.controlId: SI-7 — Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity (boot integrity)
controls.title: Boot integrity — genuine integrity failures fail-closed; transient indexing degrades safe
controls.implementation: Boot now distinguishes integrity-critical failures (fail-closed) from transient ones (warn-and-continue). Hard fail-closed: Milvus unreachable after retries → process.exit(1) (server.ts:2044-2045); DB schema/connectivity failures → process.exit(1) (server.ts:1865, 2164); route-registration and admin-portal-validation failures exit (server.ts:2280, 2401). Correctly downgraded (the prior boot-loop bug): MCP tool indexing failure and 0-tools post-index verification are warn-then-continue and re-trigger on first chat (server.ts:2058, 2064, 2074; startup/08-tool-cache.ts:94) — these are first-boot-empty states, not integrity violations. pgvector/halfvec extension is created idempotently on first postgres boot (scripts/postgres-init/01-extensions.sql per project docs). Forward-only migration integrity is itself pinned by __tests__/architecture/no-destructive-migration-at-boot.source-regression.test.ts (boot runs `prisma migrate deploy`, never `db push --accept-data-loss`).

Local users authenticate via bcrypt + HS256 JWT (routes/local-auth.ts). Confirmed: tokenValidator.ts:90 hard-codes tenantId:'local' and there is no live SSO verification path.

nist.family: CM (Configuration Management) — NIST 800-53 Rev 5, High baseline

controls.title: Baseline Configuration
controls.implementation: Configuration is captured as committed, version-controlled baselines rather than ad-hoc runtime state. The compose baseline (docker-compose.yml) is env-driven: every operationally-significant value resolves from an env var with an explicit default or a fail-fast guard (docker-compose.yml:30-300). The Helm baseline (helm/openagentic/values.yaml:1-60) is a documented single-source-of-truth values file with an explicit 'Override ... in your own values file for a real deployment' baseline note (values.yaml:11-13,25). NODE_ENV=production is pinned in BOTH baselines (docker-compose.yml:184, helm/openagentic/templates/api.yaml:49-50), so the hardened production code paths are the default deployed configuration.

controls.title: Configuration Settings (no weak committed config defaults; secrets fail-closed)
controls.implementation: COMPOSE PATH (strong): docker-compose.yml no longer ships *-change-me secret defaults — every trust-root secret uses the fail-fast `${VAR:?...}` form: POSTGRES_PASSWORD (line 32, also in DATABASE_URL line 185), JWT_SECRET (272), SIGNING_SECRET (273), INTERNAL_API_KEY (279), INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET (283). A missing value aborts `docker compose up` rather than booting with a default. RUNTIME VALIDATOR (strong): services/openagentic-api/src/config/secrets.config.ts validateSecret() fails CLOSED when NODE_ENV=production (lines 77-91) — a missing OR weak/placeholder secret THROWS and aborts boot (loadSecrets re-throws, line 214). The blocklist covers exact placeholders incl. 'change-me'/'changeme'/'dev-token' (101-112) AND substring matches incl. 'change-me','change_me','dev-','dev_' (117-127), so the historically-shipped `...-change-me` defaults are rejected anywhere in the value (129-135). Since NODE_ENV=production is pinned in both deploy paths, this guard is active in the real deployment.
controls.poam: HELM PATH GAP (real, not yet closed): helm/openagentic/values.yaml:27-31 STILL ship weak committed defaults — jwtSecret: openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me, signingSecret/internalApiKey/frontendSecret with -change-me suffixes, postgresPassword: openagentic-dev-pg-pass. These render unconditionally into a live k8s Secret (helm/openagentic/templates/secret.yaml:9-28 — NO existingSecret gate, NO guard) and into searxng (templates/searxng.yaml:12). MITIGATION that prevents an actual weak-secret boot: the api validator (NODE_ENV=production) rejects the -change-me JWT/SIGNING/INTERNAL values at boot, so the api fails closed even on a default `helm install`. RESIDUAL DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH GAP: mcp-proxy bootstrap_jwt_keys (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:119-123) only rejects keys starting with the literal 'dev-secret' — the Helm default 'openagentic-dev-jwt-secret-change-me' does NOT start with 'dev-secret', so the proxy would NOT independently reject it; it relies on the api refusing to boot. Also postgresPassword 'openagentic-dev-pg-pass' contains no blocklisted substring and would be accepted. Remaining action (new POA&M item, treat as a launch blocker for the Helm path): remove the weak literals from values.yaml (require an operator-supplied value or generate per-release), add an `existingSecret` option to secret.yaml, and broaden mcp-proxy bootstrap_jwt_keys + the api substring blocklist to cover the 'openagentic-dev-' / '-pg-pass' patterns. Tracked alongside C12.

controls.title: Least Functionality (MCP Inspector off by default; unauthenticated debug surfaces gated)
controls.implementation: The MCP Inspector — an unauthenticated debug UI — is OFF by default and opt-in only: services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:88 reads ENABLE_MCP_INSPECTOR defaulting to 'false', the subprocess only spawns inside `if ENABLE_MCP_INSPECTOR:` (main.py:369-382), and enabling it emits an explicit 'do not enable in production' warning (main.py:370). The disabled path is the default (main.py:384). The control rationale is documented inline as CM-7/SA-15 (main.py:85). Separately, the unauthenticated first-run probe-ollama route is setup-GATED: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/setup.ts:94-100 returns 409 once an admin exists (NIST AC-3 note at :95-99), so the unauthenticated surface is dead after install rather than a lingering least-functionality violation. MCP_READ_ONLY cloud guardrail exists (main.py:175) though it defaults false — see C6 POA&M.

controls.implementation: The unauthenticated probe-ollama route enforces an SSRF guard before fetching the operator-supplied host: services/openagentic-api/src/routes/setup.ts:44-67 documents the B4 (SC-7/AC-4) guard, restricts the URL scheme to http/https (:67), and blocks cloud metadata endpoints incl. 169.254.169.254 IMDS (:51). controls.title: Access Restrictions for Change (secret-commit prevention)
controls.implementation: A pre-commit hook blocks secrets from entering version control: .githooks/pre-commit:1-103 scans staged content for the real OpenAgentic key pattern `oa_(sys_)?[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}` (so bare `oa_sys_` prose is not false-flagged) at line 78, PEM private keys (BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY) at :88, and Azure AD client-secret patterns at :93-95. The scanner explicitly extends coverage to docs (RA-5 note at :46) rather than skipping markdown. This is a code-level enforcement of change-restriction, complementing branch protection.

controls.title: System Component Inventory (image/component coordinates env-driven, no embedded tenant/registry strings)
controls.implementation: Component image coordinates are env/values-driven, not hardcoded: docker-compose.yml:167 derives the image from ${OPENAGENTIC_REGISTRY:-...}/...:${OPENAGENTIC_TAG:-latest}, and helm/openagentic/values.yaml:16-19 centralizes image.registry/tag/pullPolicy as overridable values. No tenant-specific or deployment-specific namespace literals are baked into runtime config defaults; the only `<REDACTED-INTERNAL-NS>` namespace string found (services/mcps/oap-kubernetes-mcp/src/kubernetes_mcp_server/server.py:89) is inside a debug-anecdote code comment, not a configuration default — the kubernetes MCP resolves namespaces at runtime, not from a baked default. Namespace neutralization for actual config defaults holds.

controls.title: Configuration Settings — JWT algorithm pinned (alg-confusion prevention)
controls.implementation: Local-auth JWT verification pins the algorithm to prevent alg-confusion/none-alg downgrade: services/openagentic-api/src/auth/tokenValidator.ts:70 calls jwt.verify(token, JWT_SECRET, { algorithms: ['HS256'] }). On the proxy side, services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/src/main.py:799-822 only accepts an internal token when alg=='HS256' AND no 'kid' (line 799), decodes with algorithms=['HS256'] (line 822), and fails closed when the signing secret is unset (lines 806-813). The proxy also HMAC-verifies the oa_sys_ inter-service token in constant time (compute via main.py:134-144, hmac.compare_digest at :163) so the prefix alone is never trusted (the pre-B3 `Bearer oa_sys_<anything>` system-root bypass is closed — main.py:147-163,714-734), and FAILS CLOSED on a missing INTERNAL_SERVICE_SECRET (:159-161). A no-Authorization request is rejected 401 when ENABLE_AUTH (default true, main.py:83) — main.py:690-699. (This is config-hardening evidence; the IA-2 auth detail belongs to the IA family.)

CSP MCPs use static Service-Account credentials (keypair/SP/ADC), not delegated user tokens. controls.title: Developer Testing and Evaluation (incl. SAST)
controls.implementation: Static application security testing is performed by the developer/CI pipeline. SonarQube scanning is wired as a required, blocking quality gate on every push/PR to main in /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/.github/workflows/sonar.yml (job 'sonarqube', SonarSource/sonarqube-scan-action@v5 with -Dsonar.qualitygate.wait=true and -Dsonar.qualitygate.timeout=600 at lines ~38-43; SONAR_TOKEN/SONAR_HOST_URL injected from CI secrets/vars at lines ~45-47). Scan scope and new-code leak-period are configured in /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/sonar-project.properties (sonar.sources=services,tools,scripts,tests line 22; sonar.projectVersion pinned line 14 to keep the new-code gate reachable). A second commit-time architecture-invariant gate at /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/scripts/pre-commit runs the src/__tests__/architecture source-regression suite (no-hardcoded-model/secret cages). mcp-proxy auth is regression-tested (pytest 19/19 reported green, including the system-token HMAC fail-closed assertions referenced in docs/fedramp-remediation/evidence/P2-reaudit-findings.json:603-605).
controls.poam: Coverage/lcov upload to Sonar still deferred (sonar.yml line ~33); not blocking for the SAST-gate control.

controls.title: Development Process, Standards, and Tools (secure SDLC tooling)
controls.implementation: Secure-development tooling is codified in-repo: pre-commit secret gate (.githooks/pre-commit) + architecture-invariant gate (scripts/pre-commit) + Sonar SAST (.github/workflows/sonar.yml) + an OSS-integrity gate (/home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/tools/verify-oss-integrity.sh, invoked from .githooks/pre-commit tail, the build Dockerfile, and .github/workflows/oss-integrity.yml — asserts the edition flag and core install artifacts). PARTIAL: the ESLint quality gates that SA-15 expects to be enforcing remain non-blocking — per /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/docs/fedramp-remediation/ledger/P2-reaudit-gradecard.md:127 and ledger/P3-wave5-control-gaps-2.md:21, no-console/no-explicit-any are still 'warn' (~803 console.*, ~5,300 any), the C11 flip-to-error deferred.
controls.poam: C11 — flip ESLint no-console/no-explicit-any to error and make it a required CI gate; deferred post-launch (large mechanical burn-down).

controls.title: Acquisition Process — License / Provenance Correctness
controls.implementation: Licensing is correct and self-consistent for the OSS edition. Root /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/LICENSE is the genuine full Apache License 2.0 (202 lines; header 'Apache License / Version 2.0, January 2004'). The previously-fabricated/MIT-misdeclared mcp-proxy service doc is fixed: /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/README.md:558 reads 'Apache-2.0 License — see [LICENSE](../../LICENSE)', pointing at the root Apache file (de-fabrication and license correction from the prior D-grade SA-5/SR-3 finding at docs/fedramp-remediation/ledger/P2-reaudit-gradecard.md:72).

controls.title: Supply Chain Controls and Processes (build reproducibility / dependency pinning)
controls.implementation: JavaScript/TypeScript supply chain is reproducibly pinned: committed lockfiles exist across the workspace (/home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/pnpm-lock.yaml, services/openagentic-api/{pnpm-lock.yaml,package-lock.json}, services/openagentic-ui/package-lock.json, services/openagentic-proxy/package-lock.json, services/openagentic-workflows/package-lock.json, tools/setup/package-lock.json, tests/*). PARTIAL: Python dependencies are NOT pinned — /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/requirements.txt uses floating ranges (e.g. 'fastapi>=0.104.0,<1.0.0', 'boto3>=1.34.0,<2.0.0') with no requirements.lock and no --require-hashes anywhere under services/ (verified: no *.lock for the MCPs). This matches the still-open finding at docs/fedramp-remediation/evidence/P2-reaudit-findings.json:1205.
controls.poam: Generate hash-pinned Python lockfiles (pip-compile --generate-hashes → requirements.lock, install --require-hashes) for mcp-proxy and each services/mcps/oap-*-mcp; batched with C12 release-hardening.

controls.title: Component Authenticity — Base-Image Pinning (C12)
controls.implementation: Base images are NOT digest-pinned anywhere on the branch (verified: no '@sha256:' in any service Dockerfile). The 9 MCP servers and openagentic-proxy use distroless Chainguard bases but via a floating build-arg tag (e.g. services/mcps/oap-aws-mcp/Dockerfile:67 'FROM cgr.dev/chainguard/python:${PYTHON_TAG}'); the mcp-proxy rides 'python:3.11-slim' (services/openagentic-mcp-proxy/Dockerfile:1); api/workflows on 'node:22-slim'; UI runtime on 'nginx:alpine' (services/openagentic-ui/Dockerfile:129). docker-compose.yml additionally floats ollama/searxng/nginx at ':latest' (per P2-reaudit-findings.json:971). This is the deferred C12 item, correctly recorded as deferred in docs/fedramp-remediation/ledger/P3-wave5-control-gaps-2.md:22 and the gradecard C12 row (ledger/P2-reaudit-gradecard.md:128).
controls.poam: C12 — replace every FROM with FROM <image>@sha256:<digest> (and pin compose images), add a source-regression arch test failing CI on any un-digested FROM/compose image, and standardize mcp-proxy onto the distroless base. Deferred to the release-hardening pass.

controls.title: Provenance (drift / digest manifest)
controls.implementation: No build-time SBOM or @sha256 image-digest manifest is emitted and no admission/CI check verifies deployed images against an approved hashed baseline (gap recorded at docs/fedramp-remediation/evidence/P2-reaudit-findings.json:1040-1041). The sync tool (tools/sync-upstream.py) maintains source currency but does not attest the running image set.
controls.poam: Emit a per-release SBOM + resolved-@sha256 digest manifest and a CI/admission check; paired with the C12 digest-pinning POA&M.

controls.title: Risk Assessment (POA&M as risk-response inputs)
controls.implementation: A structured, in-repo risk/remediation ledger substantiates the assessment process: /home/trent/agenticwork/openagentic/docs/fedramp-remediation/ contains the wave ledgers (ledger/P3-wave4-control-gaps.md, ledger/P3-wave5-control-gaps-2.md) and an adversarial re-audit findings set (evidence/P2-reaudit-findings.json) mapping each gap to its NIST control, file:line gap, and remediation. The deferred items are explicitly enumerated as deliberate, documented risk acceptances (P3-wave5-control-gaps-2.md:19-22 lists C6/C11/C12). Note the formal organizational POA&M itself is an ATO-package artifact maintained outside the source tree (per P2-reaudit-findings.json:1265-1267); this repo provides the technical evidence feeding it.

controls.title: External Identity Provider / Delegated-Token Supply (federated identity)
controls.implementation: N/A — the OSS edition is LOCAL-AUTH ONLY. AAD IdP / OBO / Google-SSO were excised entirely (commit 0e20c668b on this branch); CSP MCPs authenticate via static Service Accounts (keypair/SP/ADC), so there is no federated identity provider, delegated-token (OBO), or external-IdP supply-chain surface to assess.
